document_metadata.title: MSCI Fundamental Data Methodology
sections: 4 Fundamental Data Treatments for Corporate Events
6 Hyperinflationary Economies - Adjustment of Fundamental Data
Appendix I: MSCI's Treatment of Some Specific Aspects of IFRS
Appendix II: Index Ratio Calculation Examples

mandatory_fields.name: number_of_shares_outstanding
mandatory_fields.description: Total shares at company level at date of results; all classes aggregated; options/warrants/convertibles excluded; treasury shares excluded

mandatory_fields.name: trailing_12m_earnings
mandatory_fields.description: Last reported fiscal period figure plus current interim minus comparative interim

mandatory_fields.name: trailing_12m_sales
mandatory_fields.description: Net operating revenues from ongoing lines; excise tax deducted; Banks (GICS 4010) not published

mandatory_fields.name: annualized_dps
mandatory_fields.description: Sum of declared regular cash distributions over latest 12 months; or frequency-adjusted for US/Canada

mandatory_fields.name: book_value_per_share
mandatory_fields.description: Latest shareholders equity excluding minority interest, treasury shares, non-equity preferred

preprocessing.formula: Last fiscal period figure + (current interim - comparative interim)
exceptions: BVPS: latest reported, no trailing calculation

preprocessing.formula: Trailing 12m figure / shares outstanding at company level
exceptions: US, Canada, UK, Ireland: average shares for EPS, CEPS, SPS

new_rules.name: exclude_stale_fundamental_data_18m
new_rules.purpose: Exclude per-share and ratio calculations when the gap between calculation date and financial reporting period end exceeds 18 months, preventing use of outdated fundamentals.

source.markdown: MSCI calculates security per share and ratios if the gap between calculation date and financial reporting period end date is less than 18 months.

business: no security in output has reporting gap exceeding 18 months

new_rules.name: use_average_shares_us_canada_uk_ireland
new_rules.purpose: Apply average number of shares (not period-end) for EPS, CEPS, and SPS per-share calculations for US, Canada, UK, and Ireland, matching local standard practice.

source.markdown: except United States of America, Canada, the United Kingdom and Ireland where average number of shares is used for per share calculations of earnings, cash earnings, and sales

business: shares denominator for EPS/CEPS/SPS equals average_shares_outstanding when country in {US, CA, GB, IE}

new_rules.name: compute_eps12f
new_rules.purpose: Compute 12-month forward EPS on a rolling basis by blending current-year and next-year consensus forecasts weighted by months remaining in fiscal year; fall back to EPS1 alone when EPS2 is unavailable and M >= 8.

source.markdown: EPS12F = (M x EPS1 + (12-M) x EPS2) / 12 ... For cases where EPS2 is not available and M is greater than or equal to 8, EPS1 is used as an approximation of EPS12F.

technical: eps1_consensus_forecast present for all rows with eps12f output
business: when eps2_consensus_forecast is null and months_to_fiscal_year_end >= 8, eps12f equals eps1_consensus_forecast

new_rules.name: compute_eps12b
new_rules.purpose: Compute 12-month backward EPS by blending last reported fiscal EPS with the current-year consensus estimate, mirroring the EPS12F formula but anchored on historical results.

source.markdown: EPS12B = (M x EPS0 + (12-M) x EPS1) / 12 ... EPS0 is last fiscal year end reported earnings per share

technical: eps0_last_fiscal_year_eps present for all rows with eps12b output
business: eps12b is a weighted blend of eps0 and eps1 with weights M/12 and (12-M)/12 respectively

new_rules.name: compute_egro_ols
new_rules.purpose: Compute 5-year historical EPS growth trend (EGRO) as OLS regression slope of annual EPS over cumulative months, divided by the mean of absolute EPS values; require at least four comparable annual EPS data points.

source.markdown: A minimum of the last four comparable EPS or SPS values is needed to compute the historical growth trends. Growth trends for securities without at least four years of comparable EPS or SPS data are not calculated

technical: five_year_eps_history has at least MIN_COMPARABLE_YEARS non-null entries when egro is populated
business: egro is null when fewer than 4 comparable EPS values exist
egro equals OLS slope of EPS on cumulative months divided by mean absolute EPS

new_rules.name: compute_sgro_ols
new_rules.purpose: Compute 5-year historical SPS growth trend (SGRO) as OLS regression slope of annual SPS over cumulative months, divided by the mean of absolute SPS values; require at least four comparable annual SPS data points.

source.markdown: A minimum of the last four comparable EPS or SPS values is needed to compute the historical growth trends. Growth trends for securities without at least four years of comparable EPS or SPS data are not calculated

technical: five_year_sps_history has at least MIN_COMPARABLE_YEARS non-null entries when sgro is populated
business: sgro is null when fewer than 4 comparable SPS values exist
sgro equals OLS slope of SPS on cumulative months divided by mean absolute SPS

new_rules.name: compute_roe
new_rules.purpose: Compute return on equity as trailing 12-month EPS divided by latest BVPS; suppress result when book value is non-positive, the date gap between BV and earnings exceeds 18 months, BV date is newer than earnings date, or consolidation bases differ.

source.markdown: ROE is considered meaningful and calculated only if the following conditions are met: the book value is positive; the difference between the book value date and earnings date is less than 18 months; the book value date is equal to or older than the earnings date; book value and earnings are both consolidated or both non-consolidated.

technical: book_value_per_share and trailing_12m_earnings_per_share present for all roe-populated rows
business: roe is null when book_value_per_share <= 0
roe is null when abs(book_value_period_date - earnings_period_date) > 18 months
roe is null when book_value_period_date > earnings_period_date
roe is null when consolidation bases differ

new_rules.name: compute_egrsf
new_rules.purpose: Compute short-term forward EPS growth rate as (EPS12F - EPS12B) / |EPS12B|, measuring expected 12-month earnings growth.

source.markdown: EGRSF = (EPS12F - EPS12B) / |EPS12B|
source.section: 2.2.5 Short-Term Forward Earnings Per Share Growth Rate (EGRSF)

technical: eps12f and eps12b both present when egrsf is populated
business: egrsf is null when eps12b is zero or null

new_rules.name: exclude_egrlf_single_contributor_outlier
new_rules.purpose: Suppress EGRLF when the consensus estimate is an outlier (greater than 50% or less than -33%) and is based on only one analyst contributor.

source.markdown: In cases where the LT fwd EPS G is considered an outlier (greater than 50% or less than -33%) and the corresponding estimate is based on only one contributor, the variable will not be used by MSCI.
source.section: 2.2.6 Long-Term Forward Earnings Per Share Growth Rate (EGRLF)

technical: egrlf_contributor_count present when egrlf_value is populated
business: egrlf_value is null when egrlf_value > 0.50 and egrlf_contributor_count == 1
egrlf_value is null when egrlf_value < -0.33 and egrlf_contributor_count == 1

new_rules.name: compute_evar
new_rules.purpose: Compute earnings variability as the standard deviation of year-on-year EPS growth over the last 5 fiscal years; requires at least 5 comparable EPS values; sign of denominator follows sign of prior-year EPS; growth null when prior-year EPS is zero.

source.markdown: A minimum of the last five comparable EPS values are needed to compute the earnings variability

technical: five_year_eps_history has 5 non-null entries when evar is populated
business: evar is null when fewer than 5 comparable EPS values exist
yoy_eps_growth is null when prior-year EPS is zero
yoy_eps_growth sign is flipped when prior-year EPS is negative

new_rules.name: compute_dps_annualized
new_rules.purpose: Annualize DPS as trailing 12m declared regular cash distributions; on distribution frequency change, use latest 6m only if it yields a higher annualized amount than prior; for US and Canada, multiply latest payment by distribution frequency.

source.markdown: if the regular cash distribution frequency is changed... MSCI estimates the current annualized dividend by using the latest 6 months of distribution if resulting in a higher amount than the previous annualized dividend. If the latest 6 months of distribution is lower than the previous annualized dividend, MSCI keeps the previous annualized dividend... In the US and Canada, however, regular cash distributions are annualized by multiplying the latest cash distributions by the frequency

business: when frequency_changed and country not in {US, CA}: annualized_dps = max(2 * cash_distributions_last_6m, prior_annualized_dps)
when country in {US, CA}: annualized_dps = latest_cash_distribution * payment_frequency
special dividends excluded unless paid for three consecutive years
extraordinary capital repayments excluded

new_rules.name: apply_paf_to_per_share_data_nominal_event
new_rules.purpose: Adjust all fundamental per-share data by the price adjustment factor for nominal capital structure changes (no change in market cap), ensuring per-share figures remain consistent with the adjusted price; also applies to combined nominal and real events.

source.markdown: Nominal changes in capital structure, events that have no effect on the capitalization of a security, require fundamental data to be adjusted in the same way that the price of the security is adjusted using the price adjustment factor (PAF).

business: post-event per_share_fundamental_data equals pre-event value multiplied by price_adjustment_factor
security and index valuation ratios remain neutral to nominal events

new_rules.name: suppress_small_event_dps_adjustment
new_rules.purpose: Do not adjust the current dividend rate when the PAF from a nominal or combined nominal and real capital structure event falls within 0.80 to 1.25, assuming the event will not change dividend policy.

source.markdown: In the case of the current dividend rate, if the adjustment factor is between 0.8 and 1.25, no adjustment is made. MSCI assumes that any event that leads to an adjustment factor within this range will typically not lead to a change in a company's dividend policy.

business: annualized_dps is unchanged when PAF_LOWER_BOUND <= price_adjustment_factor <= PAF_UPPER_BOUND

new_rules.name: compute_proforma_bvps_real_event
new_rules.purpose: Compute pro forma BVPS for real capital structure changes by blending existing book value with capital raised at issue price, divided by post-event total shares.

source.markdown: Pro forma book value per share is calculated using the following formula: (Latest BVPS x Latest number of company shares outstanding) + (Number of shares issued x Issue price) / Number of shares after private placement or public offering

business: proforma_bvps = (latest_bvps * shares_outstanding_pre_event + shares_issued * issue_price) / (shares_outstanding_pre_event + shares_issued)

new_rules.name: apply_loss_per_share_restatement
new_rules.purpose: When a real or combined capital structure event increases shares and earnings are negative, recalculate loss per share using post-event shares to prevent artificial amplification of losses in P/E and P/CE index calculations.

source.markdown: If earnings/cash earnings are negative, an adjustment factor is applied according to the change in number of shares (Pre-event number of shares/ Post-event number of shares) in order to avoid increasing losses artificially in P/E and P/CE calculations at index level.

business: loss_per_share is recalculated using post_event shares when trailing_12m_earnings < 0 and shares_outstanding_post_event > shares_outstanding_pre_event

new_rules.name: mark_sales_noncomparable_on_large_event
new_rules.purpose: Mark the post-event fiscal year as non-comparable in sales and earnings, dropping the 5-year growth rate calculation, when a corporate event causes annual sales to increase by more than 50% or decrease by more than 33% and the variation is not due to normal operations.

source.markdown: Whenever post event annual sales increase by more than 50% or decrease by less than 33% and The variation is determined not to be due to normal continuous business operations... MSCI will mark the post event fiscal year reporting as non-comparable in sales and earnings against previous fiscals and drop the 5 year growth rate calculation.

business: egro and sgro are null for the affected security in the post-event fiscal year when comparability flag is set
MSCI waits for post-event annual sales before marking non-comparable when only interim sales are published

new_rules.name: apply_proforma_fundamental_business_structure_change
new_rules.purpose: Use pro forma post-event fundamental data for style review when a significant M&A, spin-off inclusion, or IPO changes full market cap or shares outstanding by 50% or more increase or 33% or more decrease, provided the post-event entity moves between specified size segments.

source.markdown: MSCI uses a threshold of an increase of 50% or greater or a decrease of 33% or more, relative to the company's full market capitalization before the event to identify such significant events.

business: style review performed only when security moves from non-constituent to GIMI constituent, or from Small Cap to Standard
pro forma fundamental data used until company reports post-event actuals

new_rules.name: apply_proforma_for_loss_making_acquisition
new_rules.purpose: Use estimated pro forma combined fundamental data for an acquirer when it acquires a loss-making Standard Index constituent whose trailing 12-month losses exceeded USD 1 billion at month-end before the ex-date.

source.markdown: The acquired loss-making company was a constituent of the MSCI Standard Size Segment before the acquisition; and The 12-month trailing losses of the acquired loss-making company exceeded USD 1bn at the end of the month previous to the ex-date of the acquisition event.

business: pro forma fundamental data applied until acquirer reports post-event actual results
both trailing loss threshold and Standard constituent status required

new_rules.name: no_adjustment_for_buyback_special_dividend_debt_swap
new_rules.purpose: Make no fundamental data adjustment for share buybacks, special cash dividends, or debt-to-equity swaps; these event types are treated as pass-through by default.

source.markdown: For events such as share buybacks, special cash dividends, and debt-to-equity swaps, no adjustment is made generally.
source.section: 4 Fundamental Data Treatments for Corporate Events

business: per_share_fundamental_data is unchanged when event_type in {share_buyback, special_cash_dividend, debt_to_equity_swap}

new_rules.name: apply_ifrs_goodwill_amortization_adjustment
new_rules.purpose: Add back goodwill amortization to pre-IFRS historical earnings to maintain 5-year EPS comparability when IFRS adoption changes reported earnings by 10% or more.

source.markdown: MSCI will adjust historical [non-IFRS] earnings by adding back 'amortization of goodwill' for companies where the difference between IFRS earnings and non-IFRS earnings is more than or equal to 10%.
source.section: Appendix I: MSCI's Treatment of Some Specific Aspects of IFRS

technical: historical_earnings_ifrs and historical_earnings_non_ifrs both present for adjustment
business: adjusted pre-IFRS earnings = historical_earnings_non_ifrs + goodwill_amortization when abs((ifrs - non_ifrs) / non_ifrs) >= 0.10
previously published financial data is not revised; only internal comparability is adjusted

new_rules.name: adjust_trailing_eps_real_estate_revaluation
new_rules.purpose: Adjust trailing EPS and five-year historical EPS for Real Estate companies (GICS Sector 60) by excluding gains or losses from revaluation of investment property, treating them as non-recurring.

source.markdown: 'Revaluation of property' can have a large impact on earnings of Real Estate companies. Therefore, MSCI adjusts the earnings of real estate companies (i.e., companies falling under the GICS Industry Group 'Real Estate' for 'Gain / Loss due to Revaluation of Investment Property'). To compute the trailing EPS of a real estate company that has opted to revalue its investment property, MSCI adjusts the trailing EPS for the 'Gain / Loss due to Revaluation of Investment Property' for the latest trailing period updated by MSCI.
source.section: Appendix I: MSCI's Treatment of Some Specific Aspects of IFRS

technical: property_revaluation_gain_loss present for GICS Sector 60 securities
business: adjusted_trailing_eps = trailing_eps - property_revaluation_gain_loss for GICS Sector 60 securities reporting under IFRS from December 2004 onwards
five-year historical EPS adjusted analogously for growth-trend comparability

new_rules.name: announce_security_yield_correction_above_threshold
new_rules.purpose: Trigger a security-level announcement with revised valuation ratios when an error causes the incorrect security yield to deviate from the correct yield by more than 0.25 percent (0.5 percent for book value yield); historical security data is always revised regardless of magnitude.

source.markdown: If the incorrect security yields are within +/-0.25% of the correct yields (+/-0.5% for book value yield), no revision is done on the country and industry index. No announcement is made by MSCI. Per share figures and valuation ratios at security level are revised in the MSCI historical database. If the incorrect security yields are not within +/-0.25% of the correct yields (+/-0.5% for book value yield), an announcement is made by MSCI with the revised valuation ratios at the security level.

technical: correct and incorrect security yield values available for comparison
business: historical security-level valuation ratios are always revised regardless of impact magnitude
security-level announcement triggered when abs(yield_error) > SECURITY_YIELD_THRESHOLD (or SECURITY_BV_YIELD_THRESHOLD for book value yield)

new_rules.name: apply_index_revision_above_yield_threshold
new_rules.purpose: Trigger recalculation and announcement of index-level valuation ratios only when the error in country or industry index yield exceeds 0.1 percent (0.5 percent for book value yield); below threshold, no index revision or announcement is made.

source.markdown: If the incorrect index yields at country/industry level are within +/-0.1% of the correct yields (+/-0.5% for book value yield), no index revision is done. No announcement is made by MSCI. If the incorrect index yields are not within +/-0.1 of the correct yield (+/-0.5% for book value yield), new valuation ratios are calculated, for all the Indexes in which the security is included.

technical: correct and incorrect index yield values available for comparison
business: no index action when abs(index_yield_error) <= INDEX_YIELD_THRESHOLD (or INDEX_BV_YIELD_THRESHOLD for book value yield)
index-level announcement and revision triggered when abs(index_yield_error) exceeds threshold

new_rules.name: apply_hyperinflationary_currency_substitution
new_rules.purpose: Publish fundamental data in a relatively stable foreign currency for companies domiciled in hyperinflationary economies, identified by cumulative inflation approaching or exceeding 100% over three years.

source.markdown: The cumulative inflation rate over three years is approaching, or exceeds, 100%... If a country qualifies as a hyperinflationary economy, MSCI may opt to publish the fundamental data and all other security ratios and growth rates in a relatively more stable foreign currency
source.section: 6 Hyperinflationary Economies - Adjustment of Fundamental Data

business: reporting currency overridden to stable foreign currency when country_cumulative_inflation_3yr >= CUMULATIVE_INFLATION_THRESHOLD

mock_datapoints.name: security_id
mock_datapoints.description: Unique security identifier.

mock_datapoints.name: weight
mock_datapoints.description: Index weight from base universe, normalised to sum to 1.

mock_datapoints.name: country_of_classification
mock_datapoints.description: Country of classification per GIMI Methodology; determines reporting currency and share-count methodology.
definition_notes: US, CA, GB, IE use average shares for EPS/CEPS/SPS
hyperinflationary countries may have reporting currency overridden to a stable foreign currency

mock_datapoints.name: gics_industry_group
mock_datapoints.description: GICS Industry Group code; controls which per-share figures are published (Banks 4010: no SPS or CEPS; Insurance 4030: no CEPS).
definition_notes: Banks (4010): SPS not published; CEPS not published
Insurance (4030): CEPS not published; net premiums earned used as sales proxy
Real Estate (GICS Sector 60, industry groups 6010/6020): property revaluation gains/losses treated as non-recurring for EPS

mock_datapoints.name: number_of_shares_outstanding
mock_datapoints.description: Total shares at company level at date of results; all classes including unlisted aggregated; stock options, warrants, and convertibles excluded; treasury shares excluded.
definition_notes: preferred shares with equity-like characteristics included
ADR/GDR/CPO securities: per-share data reported on per-DR basis

mock_datapoints.name: average_shares_outstanding
mock_datapoints.description: Average shares outstanding over the reporting period; used instead of period-end shares for EPS, CEPS, SPS in US, Canada, UK, Ireland.

mock_datapoints.name: consolidation_basis
mock_datapoints.description: Basis on which fundamental data is reported; MSCI uses consolidated data by default, non-consolidated only when consolidated is unavailable.
definition_notes: in countries like India interim results may be non-consolidated while fiscal-period results are consolidated
ROE requires earnings and book value on the same consolidation basis

mock_datapoints.name: trailing_12m_earnings
mock_datapoints.description: Net income from continuing operations available to all equity shareholders, excluding extraordinary items, minority interest, and preferred dividends; adjusted for non-recurring items.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: net income from the continuing operations available to all equity shareholders excluding extraordinary items or non-recurring items
definition_notes: Real Estate (GICS Sector 60): property revaluation gains/losses excluded
Oil & Gas (GICS Industry Group 1010): impairment of properties not excluded
Banks (4010) and Insurance (4030): unrealized gains/losses on financial assets not excluded
US/CA/UK/IE: uses basic undiluted EPS from continuing operations as reported

mock_datapoints.name: trailing_12m_sales
mock_datapoints.description: Net operating revenues from all ongoing lines of business; excise tax deducted; Banks (GICS 4010) excluded; Insurance (4030) uses net premiums earned.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: net operating revenues from all on-going lines of business
definition_notes: Banks (4010): SPS not published
Insurance (4030): net premiums earned used as proxy
cumulative sales used, not quarterly
excise tax always deducted when available

mock_datapoints.name: trailing_12m_cash_earnings
mock_datapoints.description: Earnings plus depreciation and amortization as reported; where amortization not reported, only depreciation added; latest fiscal D&A used if not in interim statements.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: earnings per share including depreciation and amortization as reported by the company
definition_notes: Banks (4010) and Insurance (4030): CEPS not published

mock_datapoints.name: annualized_dps
mock_datapoints.description: Annualized dividend per share; gross before withholding tax; excludes special dividends unless paid three consecutive years; excludes extraordinary capital repayments.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: sum of all the declared regular cash distributions including the ones not yet ex or paid over the latest 12-month period
definition_notes: US/CA: latest cash distribution multiplied by payment frequency
frequency change: take latest 6m only if annualized amount is higher than prior
regular capital repayments treated as regular cash dividends
yields gross, withholding tax credits included when applicable

mock_datapoints.name: book_value_per_share
mock_datapoints.description: Shareholders equity at latest period end excluding minority interest, treasury shares, and preferred shares without equity-like characteristics.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: shareholders' equity available to shareholders at the latest period end date excluding minority interest, treasury shares and preferred shares that do not exhibit equity like characteristics
definition_notes: not a trailing 12m figure; always latest reported
used for P/BV, ROE, and internal growth rate

mock_datapoints.name: financial_period_end_date
mock_datapoints.description: End date of the company's most recent reported financial period.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: gap between calculation date and financial reporting period end date is less than 18 months

mock_datapoints.name: eps1_consensus_forecast
mock_datapoints.description: Consensus analysts EPS estimate for the current fiscal year from Thomson I/B/E/S (Toyo Keizai for Japan securities not covered by I/B/E/S).
definition_notes: current fiscal year is the fiscal year following the last year with published results
refreshed monthly

mock_datapoints.name: eps2_consensus_forecast
mock_datapoints.description: Consensus analysts EPS estimate for the next fiscal year from Thomson I/B/E/S.
definition_notes: when null and months_to_fiscal_year_end >= 8, eps12f falls back to eps1_consensus_forecast

mock_datapoints.name: months_to_fiscal_year_end
mock_datapoints.description: Number of months remaining before the current fiscal year end (M in EPS12F/EPS12B formulas).

mock_datapoints.name: eps0_last_fiscal_year_eps
mock_datapoints.description: EPS from the last fully reported fiscal year; used as EPS0 in EPS12B calculation.
definition_notes: if missing from the data vendor, MSCI's last reported fiscal year EPS is used

mock_datapoints.name: egrlf_value
mock_datapoints.description: Long-term forward EPS growth rate; consensus of analysts estimates typically for 3 to 5 years; suppressed when outlier with single contributor.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.2.6 Long-Term Forward Earnings Per Share Growth Rate (EGRLF)
definition_notes: used at index level via market-cap-weighted aggregation

mock_datapoints.name: egrlf_contributor_count
mock_datapoints.description: Number of analyst contributors to the EGRLF consensus estimate.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.2.6 Long-Term Forward Earnings Per Share Growth Rate (EGRLF)

mock_datapoints.name: five_year_eps_history
mock_datapoints.description: List of up to 5 annual fiscal year-end EPS values used for EGRO and EVAR calculations.
definition_notes: MSCI may restate historical EPS for corporate events to maintain comparability
minimum 4 comparable values for EGRO; minimum 5 for EVAR

mock_datapoints.name: five_year_sps_history
mock_datapoints.description: List of up to 5 annual fiscal year-end SPS values used for SGRO calculation.
definition_notes: minimum 4 comparable values required
MSCI may restate for corporate events

mock_datapoints.name: price_adjustment_factor
mock_datapoints.description: Price adjustment factor (PAF) applied at the ex-date of a capital structure event; ratio of ex-price to cum-price.
definition_notes: values within [0.80, 1.25] exempt dividend rate adjustment
when event increases shares the PAF decreases per-share figures; when event decreases shares the PAF increases per-share figures

mock_datapoints.name: country_cumulative_inflation_3yr
mock_datapoints.description: Cumulative consumer price inflation for the country over the most recent 3-year period; triggers hyperinflationary treatment at or near 100%.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: cumulative inflation rate over three years is approaching, or exceeds, 100%

mock_datapoints.name: property_revaluation_gain_loss
mock_datapoints.description: Gain or loss from revaluation of investment property reported by Real Estate companies (GICS Sector 60); excluded from MSCI trailing and historical EPS as a non-recurring item.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix I: MSCI's Treatment of Some Specific Aspects of IFRS
definition_notes: adjustment applied to IFRS-reporting fiscals from December 2004 onwards for growth-trend comparability

final_asserts: every security in output has reporting gap between calculation date and financial period end date strictly less than 18 months
valuation ratios are neutral to nominal-only capital structure events when pre-event per-share figures are positive

external_references.section: 1.2.1 Sales Per Share, 1.2.2 Earnings Per Share, 1.2.3 Cash Earnings Per Share
4 Fundamental Data Treatments for Corporate Events
external_references.item: fallback policy for non-enumerated corporate events

scoped_out.quote: MSCI began publishing annual fundamental data for developed market companies in 1969 and for emerging market companies in 1988.
scoped_out.concern: Introduction and historical coverage table describe publication history, not transformation rules.

scoped_out.quote: This appendix provides examples on how index ratios are calculated.
scoped_out.concern: Appendix II contains only numeric worked examples illustrating formulas defined in sections 3.1 and 3.2. No additional rules.

scoped_out.quote: The following sections have been updated since July 2017
scoped_out.concern: Methodology Book Tracked Changes is a changelog with no rule content.

scoped_out.quote: MSCI calculates two types of security ratios: Valuation Ratios, Other Financial Ratios
scoped_out.concern: Sections 2.1 Valuation Ratios and 2.2.3 Payout Ratio, 2.2.4 Current Internal Growth Rate (g), 2.2.8 through 2.2.42 define ratio formulas as pure arithmetic on defined datapoints with no eligibility, selection, weighting, or event thresholds beyond the general 18-month staleness rule already captured.

scoped_out.quote: Index ratios computed by MSCI result from the aggregation of the constituent using the methodology that best reflects the underlying index.
scoped_out.concern: Section 3.1 Index Valuation Ratios and section 3.2 Other Index Financial Ratios (including 3.2.8 Estimated Daily Dividend Yield) define aggregation mechanics (inclusion-factor-weighted price-to-fundamental aggregation and a daily dividend-yield formula) that are computation specifications, not pipeline rules with conditional thresholds.

scoped_out.quote: MSCI covers the following Balance Sheet items in its product files.
scoped_out.concern: Section 5 defines balance-sheet item coverage (FA, INV, OA, CA, BV, OL, LD, CL) and ratio formulas (MLEV, BLEV, DTOA). These are data product specifications and formula definitions; no conditional thresholds.

clarifications_needed.quote: for corporate events not described in this section or combinations of different types of corporate events and other exceptional cases, MSCI will determine the most appropriate implementation method and will announce it prior to the changes becoming effective
clarifications_needed.concern: Section 4 intro: fallback for non-enumerated corporate events is fully discretionary. No algorithmic rule can be derived.

clarifications_needed.quote: MSCI will review the inflation rates of countries on a periodic basis to identify cases that meet the above threshold.
clarifications_needed.concern: Section 6: review frequency for hyperinflationary classification is not specified; cannot determine whether annual, semi-annual, or event-driven.

clarifications_needed.quote: In order to calculate the short-term forward EPS growth rate, if the EPS of the last reported fiscal year is missing from the data vendor, then MSCI's last reported fiscal year EPS will be used.
clarifications_needed.concern: Section 1.3: the distinction between vendor-supplied EPS0 and MSCI's own last reported fiscal year EPS is not defined, nor under what circumstances MSCI's figure differs from the vendor's.

clarifications_needed.quote: In order to provide meaningful historical growth trends, MSCI may restate EPS and SPS to reflect the impact of corporate events that may affect historical financial data on an on-going basis.
clarifications_needed.concern: Sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.7: restatement of historical EPS/SPS for corporate events is discretionary ('may restate'). No algorithmic rule specifies which events trigger restatement or the restatement methodology.

clarifications_needed.quote: 2) In order to make sales comparable, MSCI will make case-by-case adjustments.
clarifications_needed.concern: Appendix I point 2: IFRS sales comparability adjustments are case-by-case. No threshold or formula extractable.

clarifications_needed.quote: in countries like India, owing to the disclosure procedures, there could be use of unconsolidated data for interim financial results whereas the fiscal period financial results could be consolidated.
clarifications_needed.concern: Section 1.1.2: country-level consolidation-basis exception is described only by example (India). Scope of exception (which other countries, which interim types) is undefined.

clarifications_needed.quote: For certain companies that report higher revenues on account of notional contracts or sales, MSCI may report revenues net of operating cost.
clarifications_needed.concern: Section 1.2.1: identification of 'certain companies' with notional-contract revenue is discretionary; no criteria or list is provided.
